title: Protect Funding for Women's Health Care Act
short_title: Protect Funding for Women's Health Care Act
plain_summary: This bill would stop the federal government from giving money to Planned Parenthood and any organizations connected to it. The government currently provides funding to Planned Parenthood to help pay for health care services for women. If this bill becomes law, that money would no longer go to Planned Parenthood, but other doctors and health care providers could still get federal money to provide women's health services. The bill's supporters say other health care providers will be able to take care of women who currently go to Planned Parenthood.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
subjects: Social work, volunteer service, charitable organizations

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protect Funding for Women's Health Care Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has found these facts. State and county health departments, community health centers, hospitals, doctor offices, and other groups currently provide health services to women and will keep providing them. These health services include lab tests, X-rays, well-child care, care during and after pregnancy, shots to prevent disease, family planning services including birth control, testing for diseases spread through sex, cancer screenings for the cervix and breasts, and referrals to other doctors. Many of these groups provide services to all people, no matter if they can pay or not. They also provide services in areas where there are not enough doctors and to groups of people who have trouble getting medical care. All money that is no longer available to Planned Parenthood Federation of America will still be made available to other qualified groups to provide women's health care services.

sections.title: Section 3  Prohibition
sections.text: Even if other laws say something different, no federal money can be given to Planned Parenthood Federation of America or to any of its partner organizations, smaller companies it owns, groups that take its place, or clinics. Nothing in this law should be understood to change any rules in spending laws about abortion. Nothing in this law should be understood to reduce the total amount of federal money available to support women's health.
